prompt: Which Renaissance artist painted the Mona Lisa?
The Eiffel Tower was originally built as the entrance arch for which event?
Which two primary colours do you mix to make orange?
The famous sculpture of a man sitting and thinking, head resting on his fist, is called what?
Vincent van Gogh sold only ONE painting during his entire lifetime. What was it called?
The Art Nouveau movement got its name from a shop in which city?
Which Japanese art form — a centuries-old practice of folding paper into animals and geometric shapes — literally translates as "paper folding"?
The Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao, Spain — one of the most celebrated buildings of the 20th century — was designed by which architect?
Frida Kahlo painted many self-portraits because she spent long periods bedridden after a bus accident. What unusual feature did she include in almost all of them?
options: A mirror painted into the background
A pet monkey sitting on her shoulder
Flowers woven directly into her painted hair

prompt: The Pantone colour-matching system assigns numbers to specific hues. What colour is Pantone 448 C, officially voted the world's ugliest colour and used on cigarette packaging to deter smokers?
The ancient Egyptians used a blue pigment so prized that its formula was lost for centuries. What is it called?
Which radical design school, founded in Weimar, Germany in 1919, united fine art with craft and industrial production — and was shut down by the Nazis in 1933?
Which Dutch artist is famous for paintings of swirling skies and sunflowers, and for cutting off part of his own ear?
What do we call a painting of a bowl of fruit, flowers, or other objects that don't move?
Which famous melting-clocks painting was created by Salvador Dalí?
The ceiling of the Sistine Chapel, showing God reaching out to Adam, was painted by which artist?
What is the name of the flat board an artist holds to mix paint colours while working?
Andy Warhol made a famous pop-art print of cans of which everyday product?
On the artist's colour wheel, which colour sits directly opposite blue as its 'complementary' colour?
The Statue of Liberty was a gift to the United States from which country?
Edvard Munch's 'The Scream' shows a figure clutching its face on a bridge under a sky of which dominant colour?
Which movement, named after a Monet painting, was first mocked by critics as the work of unfinished 'impressions'?
In typography, what is the small decorative stroke at the ends of letters in fonts like Times New Roman called?
Pablo Picasso co-founded a movement that broke objects into geometric shapes seen from many angles at once. What was it called?
Which artist is best known for large 'drip paintings' made by flinging and pouring paint onto canvas laid on the floor?
The Terracotta Army — thousands of life-sized clay soldiers — was buried to guard the tomb of the first emperor of which country?
After Banksy's 'Girl with Balloon' shredded itself moments after selling at auction in 2018, the half-destroyed work was given a brand-new official title. What is it now called?
One 20th-century artist became so identified with a single deep shade that he patented his own version of it in 1960 and used it to paint blank monochrome canvases. Which artist?
In Japanese craft, 'kintsugi' repairs broken pottery by filling the cracks with lacquer dusted with which precious metal?
The RGB colour model used by screens is 'additive' — what colour do you get when red, green, and blue light are combined at full strength?
Artemisia Gentileschi was a celebrated Baroque painter known for dramatic biblical scenes like 'Judith Slaying Holofernes'. In which century did she work?
Grant Wood's 'American Gothic' shows a stern farmer holding which tool beside a woman in front of a house?
Which famous painting shows a young woman glancing over her shoulder, wearing a turban and a single large earring?
Michelangelo's enormous marble statue of a nude young man with a sling over his shoulder is known by which name?
The ancient Greek statue of a goddess famous for having no arms is called the Venus de what?
A Japanese woodblock print of a giant curling wave towering over boats, with Mount Fuji in the distance, is known as 'The Great Wave off' which place?
What is the name of the wooden three-legged stand that holds a canvas upright while an artist paints?
On a standard graphite pencil, what does the 'B' stand for in grades like 2B and 6B?
Comic-strip-style paintings of crying women and explosions, made up of big printed-looking dots, are the trademark of which pop artist?
Rembrandt's huge group portrait of a militia company, long darkened by grime so people thought it was a night scene, is popularly called what?
René Magritte painted a realistic pipe under the words 'Ceci n'est pas une pipe' — 'This is not a pipe.' What was his point?
options: The image is only a painting of a pipe, not a real one
He had quit smoking that year
It was a private joke about a friend
The pipe was painted by an assistant

prompt: Georges Seurat built his paintings entirely from tiny separate dots of colour that blend in the viewer's eye. What is this technique called?
Gustav Klimt's most famous painting, showing a couple embracing in a shimmering robe, is titled 'The Kiss'. Real flakes of which metal give it its glow?
Artists Christo and Jeanne-Claude were world-famous for doing what to landmarks and landscapes?
In painting terms, the white chalky primer brushed onto a canvas before painting — to seal it and give the paint something to grip — is called what?
Leonardo da Vinci's drawing of a man with arms and legs spread inside both a circle and a square is known as what?
The Bayeux Tapestry, nearly 70 metres long, is an embroidered strip depicting which historical event?
options: The Norman conquest of England in 1066

prompt: In 1856 an 18-year-old chemist trying to make a malaria drug accidentally created the first synthetic dye, a vivid purple. What was the colour named?
The 1990 theft from Boston's Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum remains the biggest art heist in history. To this day, what hangs where the stolen paintings were?
Conceptual artist Sol LeWitt sold many of his wall drawings not as finished pieces but as what?
options: Written instructions for others to execute on a wall
Sealed boxes opened only after his death
Photographs of walls he had painted

prompt: Sculptor Anish Kapoor caused an art-world feud by buying exclusive artistic rights to Vantablack, a coating famous for what property?
Which children's building toy, made of interlocking plastic bricks, takes its name from a Danish phrase meaning 'play well'?
What do we call a large picture or design painted directly onto a wall or ceiling?
Which contemporary Japanese artist is world-famous for covering paintings, giant pumpkins and mirrored 'Infinity Rooms' in polka dots?
The famous circular peace symbol — a line and two downward strokes inside a ring — was designed in 1958 to campaign against what?
The bold 'I ♥ NY' logo — one of the most copied designs ever — was sketched in the back of a taxi and given away for free by which graphic designer?
Which hot-tempered Baroque painter, as notorious for street brawls and a killing as for his art, pioneered a theatrical clash of deep shadow and blazing light?
The still-unfinished basilica La Sagrada Família in Barcelona, under construction since 1882, was designed by which architect?
Kazimir Malevich, the abstract pioneer who shocked the art world with a single 'Black Square', was born in Kyiv and is reclaimed as a national artist by which country?
In 2019 Maurizio Cattelan duct-taped a real banana to an art-fair wall and sold editions of it for six figures. What did he title the piece?
The soft, smoky blurring of edges and tones — the trick Leonardo da Vinci used to make the Mona Lisa's smile seem to flicker — is known by which Italian term?
